name: enchanting murphy llc
description_short: Enchanting Murphy Credit Consulting disputes credit report errors and negotiates with creditors to improve client credit scores. They operate on a results-based payment model.
description_long:
Enchanting Murphy Credit Consulting is a credit repair company offering personalized credit restoration services. The company positions itself around a partnership model, claiming to work closely with clients to understand their financial situations before developing customized plans. They emphasize a results-based business model where clients only pay after receiving tangible improvements to their credit profiles.

The company offers three core services: credit report evaluation and personalized plan development, goal-setting assistance to define specific credit improvement targets, and expert guidance with creditor negotiations on behalf of clients. Their approach focuses on identifying and disputing negative items on credit reports, removing inaccuracies, and implementing strategies to rebuild credit scores. They also educate clients on the benefits of improved credit, including access to higher credit limits, better loan approval odds, and improved negotiating power with lenders.

Enchanting Murphy distinguishes itself primarily through its performance-based pricing structure—clients pay only after receiving results rather than upfront fees. The company emphasizes partnership and commitment to client success, stating "YOUR SUCCESS IS OUR SUCCESS." They position themselves as experts in credit dispute and creditor negotiation, targeting clients who need systematic credit restoration rather than quick fixes.

The website contains significant limitations that warrant caution. The site is incomplete with multiple sections stating "website is currently being updated," including their case study and full service descriptions. No specific pricing information is provided despite claiming a results-based model. No verifiable credentials, licensing information, or regulatory disclosures appear on the site. The messaging uses general motivational language without concrete details about dispute processes, success rates, or realistic timelines. Prospective clients should request detailed written information about their fee structure and dispute methodology before engaging.
address: 40 Wall St ste 2869, New York, NY 10005

company_info.headquarters: 40 Wall St ste 2869, New York, NY 10005

pros: Results-based payment model: Clients only pay after receiving verified improvements to credit
Personalized credit evaluation and customized plan development for individual situations
Creditor negotiation services on behalf of clients
Dispute and error correction services targeting negative items on credit reports
Educational content about credit improvement benefits and financial flexibility
Extended office hours (9am-6pm Monday-Saturday) and multiple contact methods provided
Emphasis on partnership approach and long-term client success
cons: Website is significantly incomplete with multiple sections under construction, reducing transparency
No pricing information disclosed despite claims of results-based payment model
No verifiable credentials, licensing numbers, or regulatory disclosures visible on website
No case studies or success rate data provided to substantiate claims
Vague language about dispute processes and timeline expectations for credit improvements
best_for: Consumers with errors or inaccuracies on their credit reports needing formal disputes
Individuals seeking creditor negotiation support without paying upfront fees
People wanting personalized credit improvement plans rather than DIY dispute letters
diagnosis: Enchanting Murphy is positioned for consumers needing professional credit dispute and creditor negotiation services who prefer results-based payment. However, the incomplete website, lack of transparent pricing details, absent credentials, and missing success data create significant uncertainty—prospects should request comprehensive written documentation of services, fee structures, and dispute methodologies before commitment.
services: Dispute filing for errors and negative items
